title: At the Four Seasons in Damascus, power shifts but the elite remain
subhead: A symbol of Bashar al-Assad's grip on Syria, this luxury hotel, which opened in 2005 in the heart of the capital, was the scene of lavish parties and business dealings among those close to the regime, until the fall of the dictator in December 2024. For a time, it served as the headquarters of the new leaders; today, the establishment is a networking hub for entrepreneurs seeking business opportunities in a country where everything must be rebuilt.

Deal signed between Israel and Lebanon is further weakened by Israeli strikes
subhead: Lebanese Parliament Speaker and Hezbollah ally Nabih Berri said the agreement would 'not pass' in its current form, calling it a dictate that does not preserve 'Lebanon's rights,' vowing to confront it politically and warning against internal strife.

title: 250 years of American independence: The retreat from multiracial democracy
subhead: 'America 250' (7/13). A century after its founding, 38 states made up the nation. Deep divisions persisted over how to treat the Black population. Slavery had been abolished, much to the dismay of the Democrats, who were gaining ground. Although the Republicans won the presidential election, their victory came at the cost of institutionalizing segregationist policies in the Southern states.

title: 'Their mouths, eyes, hands, were taped and they were thrown into the hole': Russian soldiers confront Putin with reality of front lines
subhead: A Russian exile media outlet has analyzed nearly 7,000 letters from Russian soldiers and their families to the Kremlin thanks to a security flaw.

title: France's fantasy balls: 'We identify with the lives of castle dwellers from a bygone era'
subhead: Blending historical and fantastical references, fantasy balls promise a leap through time and a touch of magic in worlds reminiscent of the 'Bridgerton' series or 'Alice in Wonderland.' These evenings are as much about dance as they are about spectacle.

'Our infrastructure was designed for a climate that no longer exists': France's unfinished reckoning with increasing heatwaves
subhead: In 2003, an unprecedented heatwave struck France in the middle of August while the government was on vacation. It killed 15,000 people. While this public health shock transformed the alert system and the management of heat-related illnesses, France has failed to learn any lessons regarding its necessary adaptation to climate change.

title: To join the 'system' or overturn it? Far-right leader Bardella's private life and policies raise doubts
subhead: By openly seeking to appeal to economic elites and the right, far-right leader Jordan Bardella breaks with Marine Le Pen and her father's historic 'anti-system' hostility. Some party members question his desire to pursue radical policies.

title: 'The King and the Mockingbird': The animated masterpiece that inspired Studio Ghibli
subhead: His film took more than three decades to reach the screen. In 1946, Paul Grimault, then 41, began work on 'Le Roi et l'Oiseau' ('The King and the Mockingbird') with Jacques Prévert writing the screenplay. He finally saw it screened in 1979, at 74. The hand-drawn animation became a cult classic, inspiring generations of viewers and filmmakers.
